title: RFC 54 and the Origins of Open Standards in Networking

scenes.text: June 29 in the History of Computer Science: The first complete draft of the Network Control Protocol, or NCP, was published on this day in 1970 as RFC 54.
Rather than declaring a rigid standard, the authors invited open feedback from the networking community.
This collaborative process became the foundation for open standards, leading to the way Internet protocols are developed and refined together, even today.
